question.text: A scheduled commercial bank wishes to become a Trading Member (TM) on a currency derivatives exchange. Which of the following is NOT one of the eligibility criteria set by RBI?

answer.text: The bank must have a minimum paid-up capital of Rs 1,000 Cr
The bank must have a minimum net worth of Rs 500 Cr
The bank must have a Capital to Risk-Weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) of at least 10%
The bank's net NPA must not exceed 3% of net advances

explanation.text: **The Logic:** RBI specifies three key eligibility criteria for bank TMs in currency derivatives: (1) minimum net worth of Rs 500 Cr, (2) CRAR of at least 10%, and (3) net NPA not exceeding 3%. There is no requirement for Rs 1,000 Cr paid-up capital — this is a fabricated criterion. **The Trap:** The question tests whether you confuse 'paid-up capital' with 'net worth'. RBI specifies net worth (Rs 500 Cr), not paid-up capital. **Cross-Exam Context:** Compare with equity derivatives where SEBI sets CM net worth at Rs 3 Cr (L.C. Gupta) — much lower because banks are not the primary participants in equity F&O. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** These three criteria (net worth, CRAR, NPA) ensure only financially sound banks participate, protecting the exchange ecosystem from counterparty risk.
